build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: Messages arrive from field agents in real-time (or acceleratable time). Each message presents a situation and the player selects a response from two to four options. Responses have consequences that affect the operation. Score 0 if messages do not arrive, if no response options exist, or if responses have no effect. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Multiple operations run simultaneously. While handling one agent's message, others arrive and queue. The player must triage — some situations are urgent (time-limited), others can wait. Score 0 if only one operation exists at a time, if messages do not queue, or if there is no time pressure. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Agent trust ratings track reliability. Some agents may be compromised and send disinformation. The player must cross-reference reports to identify unreliable intel. Trust ratings update based on whether intel proves accurate. Score 0 if all information is always reliable, if trust ratings do not exist, or if compromised agents are absent. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Decisions have resource costs: sending backup depletes resources, extracting an agent removes them from the field, and waiting risks the window closing. A resource or budget indicator is visible. Score 0 if decisions have no cost or if resources are unlimited. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> operations briefing -> message handling -> operations conclude -> result screen showing mission outcomes and agent status. Both successful and failed operations are possible. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if operations cannot fail. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least three field agents exist with different specialties, trust levels, and personalities. Each agent has a distinct portrait and communication style. Score 0 if fewer than two agents exist or if agents are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least three simultaneous operations exist with different objectives (surveillance, extraction, sabotage). Operations have different stakes and timelines. Score 0 if only one operation exists or if operations are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Message content varies: reports, requests, warnings, and disinformation are all present. The player must distinguish reliable from unreliable intel. Score 0 if all messages are identical in type or if disinformation is absent. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Response options have meaningfully different consequences: aggressive vs cautious approaches lead to different operation outcomes. The same situation can resolve differently based on player choice. Score 0 if all responses lead to the same outcome or if choices are cosmetic. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The result screen shows detailed outcomes per operation and per agent (safe, captured, turned, killed). Different playthroughs produce different agent fates based on decisions made. Score 0 if results are generic or if agent fates are predetermined. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with cold-war espionage atmosphere (encrypted text, map pins, dossier imagery). Score 0 if it launches into raw gameplay or default UI. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The operations room shows message terminal, agent map, dossier files, and resource indicator as distinct, navigable areas. The player can access all information without confusion. Score 0 if the interface is a single undifferentiated screen or if areas overlap. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Messages, response options, agent trust ratings, operation status, and resource levels are readable at 1280x720 without overlap. Urgent messages are visually distinguished from routine ones. Score 0 if the player cannot parse available information. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Key moments have visible feedback: new messages show arrival alerts, responses show transmission effects, operation success/failure shows outcome animations, and trust changes show rating updates. Score 0 if important events have no visual feedback. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: operations room, messages, map, dossiers, and UI belong to a consistent cold-war espionage visual language with muted, tense tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Agent portraits are real illustrated or pixel-art characters with distinct appearances. The operations map uses real illustrated geography with pin markers. Dossier files look like physical documents. Score 0 if agents have no portraits or if the map is a plain shape. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The operations room background is a real illustrated environment suggesting a command center (screens, maps, filing cabinets, dim lighting). Messages have encrypted-transmission styling. Score 0 if the background is a flat color. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

UI uses styled espionage chrome: message panels have terminal/typewriter styling, response buttons have classified-document aesthetics, and transitions use static/interference effects. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
